items.title: Parallel prompt tweaks
items.scenario: You shortened the system prompt for clarity; a teammate added a few-shot example to the same paragraph.
items.explanation: Conflict. Open the file, keep both *intents*: the shorter wording AND the few-shot example. Don't just keep one side.

items.title: Both branches changed `temperature`
items.scenario: Branch A set temperature to 0.2 for an eval; branch B set it to 0.9 for diversity testing.
items.explanation: Conflict. Resolve by understanding intent — likely you want *neither value hardcoded*; refactor to a config flag and pick a sensible default.

items.title: JSONL eval data merge
items.scenario: Both branches added 50 new rows each to `evals/qa.jsonl`.
items.explanation: Treat each new row as additive — keep BOTH sides of the conflict (concatenate). Watch for accidental duplicates by ID.

items.title: Jupyter notebook conflict
items.scenario: Two branches re-ran the same notebook; outputs, execution counts and metadata all conflict.
items.explanation: Use `nbdime` for semantic notebook diffs, or set up `nbstripout` so notebook outputs never enter Git in the first place.

items.title: When abort is the right move
items.scenario: Mid-rebase you've hit 8 conflicts and they're all on a refactor that's been superseded.
items.explanation: `git rebase --abort` returns you to the pre-rebase state. Often cheaper than fighting through; merge instead, or restart the work on top of fresh `main`.
